data.content:
### Execute Lexical Data Upgrade in PayloadCMS

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/migration.mdx

Demonstrates how to use the `upgradeLexicalData` function from `@payloadcms/richtext-lexical` to automatically convert old Lexical data formats across all documents in a Payload application. This is crucial for applying breaking changes to Lexical nodes without manual intervention, ensuring all content is updated to the latest schema version. This function needs to be awaited.

```typescript
import { upgradeLexicalData } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'

await upgradeLexicalData({ payload })
```

--------------------------------

### Reinstall Project Dependencies with pnpm

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/troubleshooting/troubleshooting.mdx

After removing `node_modules` and updating `package.json` to pin exact versions, use this command to perform a clean reinstallation of all project dependencies. This ensures a fresh and consistent dependency resolution.

```bash
pnpm install
```

--------------------------------

### Importing Lexical Editor Config Utilities (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/converters.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to import `SanitizedConfig` from Payload and `editorConfigFactory` from `@payloadcms/richtext-lexical`. These imports are essential for working with Lexical editor configurations within a Payload project.

```ts
import type { SanitizedConfig } from 'payload'
import { editorConfigFactory } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'

// Your Payload Config needs to be available in order to retrieve the default editor config
const config: SanitizedConfig = {} as SanitizedConfig
```

--------------------------------

### Create Custom Lexical Editor Config with Features (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/converters.mdx

This example demonstrates creating a custom Lexical editor configuration by extending default features. It uses `editorConfigFactory.fromFeatures` to add specific features, such as `FixedToolbarFeature`, to the editor setup.

```ts
import { FixedToolbarFeature } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'

const customEditorConfig = await editorConfigFactory.fromFeatures({
  config,
  features: ({ defaultFeatures }) => [
    ...defaultFeatures,
    FixedToolbarFeature(),
  ],
})
```

--------------------------------

### Convert Simple Slate Upload Node to Lexical Format

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/migration.mdx

Demonstrates a basic converter for transforming Slate upload nodes to their Lexical equivalents. This converter extracts fields and relationship data from the Slate node and restructures it into a SerializedUploadNode format compatible with Lexical editor.

```typescript
import type { SerializedUploadNode } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'
import type { SlateNodeConverter } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'

export const SlateUploadConverter: SlateNodeConverter = {
  converter({ slateNode }) {
    return {
      type: 'upload',
      fields: {
        ...slateNode.fields,
      },
      format: '',
      relationTo: slateNode.relationTo,
      type: 'upload',
      value: {
        id: slateNode.value?.id || '',
      },
      version: 1,
    } as const as SerializedUploadNode
  },
  nodeTypes: ['upload'],
}
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Default Lexical Editor Config (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/converters.mdx

This code shows how to obtain the default Lexical editor configuration using `editorConfigFactory.default`. It requires a `SanitizedConfig` object to be passed as an argument.

```ts
const defaultEditorConfig = await editorConfigFactory.default({ config })
```

--------------------------------

### Install Payload Lexical Rich Text Editor Package

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/richtext-lexical/README.md

This command installs the `@payloadcms/richtext-lexical` package using npm, which provides the Lexical Rich Text Editor plugin for Payload CMS. This is a prerequisite for using the editor in your Payload project.

```bash
npm install @payloadcms/richtext-lexical
```

--------------------------------

### Database Version Collection Naming Convention

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/versions/overview.mdx

Shows the naming pattern for version collections in the database. When versions are enabled, a new collection is created following the pattern '_slug_versions' where 'slug' is replaced with your collection or global slug. This collection stores all version history for the parent document.

```text
_slug_versions
```

--------------------------------

### Extract Lexical Editor Config from a Field (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/converters.mdx

This snippet illustrates how to extract the editor configuration directly from an existing Lexical rich text field object. It utilizes `editorConfigFactory.fromField` and requires a field configuration object as input.

```ts
const fieldEditorConfig = editorConfigFactory.fromField({
  field: config.collections[0].fields[1],
})
```

--------------------------------

### Deduplicate pnpm Packages

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/troubleshooting/troubleshooting.mdx

Use `pnpm dedupe` as a final step to flatten the dependency tree and ensure that only a single version of a package is installed where possible. This can help resolve lingering dependency conflicts.

```bash
pnpm dedupe
```

--------------------------------

### Find Versions using Payload Local API in JavaScript

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/versions/overview.mdx

Retrieves a paginated set of versions for a collection using the Local API. Supports filtering with where queries, sorting, pagination parameters, localization options, and access control settings. Returns a paginated result set containing version documents with configurable depth for populating relationships.

```javascript
// Result will be a paginated set of Versions.
// See /docs/queries/pagination for more.
const result = await payload.findVersions({
  collection: 'posts', // required
  depth: 2,
  page: 1,
  limit: 10,
  where: {}, // pass a `where` query here
  sort: '-createdAt',
  locale: 'en',
  fallbackLocale: false,
  user: dummyUser,
  overrideAccess: false,
  showHiddenFields: true,
})
```

--------------------------------

### Extract Lexical Editor Config from Instantiated Editor (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/converters.mdx

This snippet shows how to retrieve the editor configuration from an already instantiated Lexical editor instance. While possible using `editorConfigFactory.fromEditor`, this method is generally less efficient compared to `fromFeatures` for custom configurations.

```ts
const editor = lexicalEditor({
  features: ({ defaultFeatures }) => [
    ...defaultFeatures,
    FixedToolbarFeature(),
  ],
})

const instantiatedEditorConfig = await editorConfigFactory.fromEditor({
  config,
  editor,
})
```

--------------------------------

### Manually Convert Slate Data to Lexical with Custom Converters

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/migration.mdx

Shows how to programmatically convert Slate data to Lexical format outside of the automatic migration script. This approach allows direct conversion with custom converters for specific use cases or manual data transformations.

```typescript
import { convertSlateToLexical } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical/migrate'

const lexicalData = convertSlateToLexical({
  slateData: mySlateData,
  converters: [...defaultSlateConverters, MyCustomConverter],
})
```

--------------------------------

### Find Version by ID using Payload Local API in JavaScript

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/versions/overview.mdx

Retrieves a specific version document by its unique ID using the Local API. Requires the collection slug and version ID as mandatory parameters. Supports depth control for relationship population, localization settings, and access control overrides. Returns the complete version document including the versioned data.

```javascript
// Result will be a Post document.
const result = await payload.findVersionByID({
  collection: 'posts', // required
  id: '507f1f77bcf86cd799439013', // required
  depth: 2,
  locale: 'en',
  fallbackLocale: false,
  user: dummyUser,
  overrideAccess: false,
  showHiddenFields: true,
})
```

--------------------------------

### Local API: payload.findVersions

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/versions/overview.mdx

Programmatically find and query paginated versions for a collection using the Payload Local API. This method returns a paginated set of version documents.

```APIDOC
## Local API: payload.findVersions

### Description
Programmatically find and query paginated versions for a collection using the Payload Local API. This method returns a paginated set of version documents.

### Method
Local API

### Endpoint
N/A (Local API method)

### Parameters
#### Function Parameters
- **collection** (string) - Required - The slug of the collection (e.g., 'posts').
- **depth** (number) - Optional - Depth of nested documents to populate.
- **page** (number) - Optional - The page number for pagination.
- **limit** (number) - Optional - The number of documents per page.
- **where** (object) - Optional - A `where` query to filter results.
- **sort** (string) - Optional - Field to sort by (e.g., '-createdAt').
- **locale** (string) - Optional - The locale to retrieve data in.
- **fallbackLocale** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to fallback to default locale if data is not found in specified locale.
- **user** (object) - Optional - User context for access control.
- **overrideAccess** (boolean) - Optional - Override access control checks.
- **showHiddenFields** (boolean) - Optional - Include hidden fields in the response.

### Request Example
```js
const result = await payload.findVersions({
  collection: 'posts', // required
  depth: 2,
  page: 1,
  limit: 10,
  where: {}, // pass a `where` query here
  sort: '-createdAt',
  locale: 'en',
  fallbackLocale: false,
  user: dummyUser,
  overrideAccess: false,
  showHiddenFields: true
});
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
Returns a paginated object similar to the REST API's success response for finding versions.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "docs": [
    {
      "_id": "61cf752c19cdf1b1af7b61f1",
      "parent": "61ce1354091d5b3ffc20ea6e",
      "autosave": false,
      "version": {
        "title": "My Post Title - Version 1",
        "content": "This is the first version of the post."
      },
      "createdAt": "2021-12-31T21:25:00.992Z",
      "updatedAt": "2021-12-31T21:25:00.992Z"
    }
  ],
  "totalDocs": 1,
  "limit": 10,
  "totalPages": 1,
  "page": 1,
  "pagingCounter": 1,
  "hasPrevPage": false,
  "hasNextPage": false,
  "prevPage": null,
  "nextPage": null
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Version Document Structure in JSON Format

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/versions/overview.mdx

Demonstrates the complete structure of a version document stored in the database for Collection documents. Each version includes a unique ID, parent document reference, autosave flag, the versioned document data, and timestamps. Global versions follow the same structure but omit the parent property since each global has its own versions collection.

```json
{
  "_id": "61cf752c19cdf1b1af7b61f1",
  "parent": "61ce1354091d5b3ffc20ea6e",
  "autosave": false,
  "version": {

  },
  "createdAt": "2021-12-31T21:25:00.992+00:00",
  "updatedAt": "2021-12-31T21:25:00.992+00:00"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Registering a Lexical Plugin in PayloadCMS Client Feature (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/custom-features.mdx

This TypeScript example demonstrates how to integrate a custom Lexical plugin into the PayloadCMS rich text editor. By importing the plugin and adding it to the `plugins` array within `createClientFeature`, you ensure it's loaded and operates within the Lexical editor's context. This is the standard method for enabling editor extensions in PayloadCMS.

```typescript
'use client'\n\nimport { createClientFeature } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical/client'\nimport { MyPlugin } from './plugin'\n\nexport const MyClientFeature = createClientFeature({\n  plugins: [MyPlugin],\n})
```

--------------------------------

### Find Document Version History

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/sdk/README.md

Retrieve version history for documents in a collection with optional filtering. Supports querying versions by parent document ID. Returns paginated list of document versions if versioning is enabled.

```typescript
// Find Versions operation
const result = await sdk.findVersions({
  collection: 'posts',
  where: { parent: { equals: post.id } },
})
```

--------------------------------

### Retrieve Lexical Editor Config in Payload `afterRead` Hook (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/docs/rich-text/converters.mdx

This comprehensive example demonstrates how to find a `RichTextField` within `siblingFields` in a collection's `afterRead` hook and then extract its Lexical editor configuration using `editorConfigFactory.fromField`. This pattern allows dynamic access to editor settings based on field data, providing flexibility for content processing.

```ts
import type { CollectionConfig, RichTextField } from 'payload'

import {
  editorConfigFactory,
  getEnabledNodes,
  lexicalEditor,
} from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'

export const MyCollection: CollectionConfig = {
  slug: 'slug',
  fields: [
    {
      name: 'text',
      type: 'text',
      hooks: {
        afterRead: [
          ({ siblingFields, value }) => {
            const field: RichTextField = siblingFields.find(
              (field) => 'name' in field && field.name === 'richText',
            ) as RichTextField

            const editorConfig = editorConfigFactory.fromField({
              field,
            })

            // Now you can use the editor config

            return value
          },
        ],
      },
    },
    {
      name: 'richText',
      type: 'richText',
      editor: lexicalEditor(),
    },
  ],
}
```

--------------------------------

### Find Specific Global Document Version by ID

Source: https://github.com/payloadcms/payload/blob/main/packages/sdk/README.md

Retrieve a specific version of a global document by version ID and global slug. Returns the complete snapshot of the global at that version. Requires versioning to be enabled for the global.

```typescript
// Find Global Version by ID operation
const result = await sdk.findGlobalVersionByID({ id: version.id, slug: 'global' })
```
